claim.content: YbRh2Si2 exhibits strong local electronic correlations and Kondo-driven heavy quasiparticles (manifest experimentally as large Sommerfeld coefficients and strongly renormalized effective masses), and the conventional local-density approximation within density-functional theory does not capture dynamic local correlations and Kondo renormalization; consequently, LDA is inadequate to quantitatively reproduce the experimentally observed Fermi-surface cross sections and large quasiparticle masses in YbRh2Si2, and many-body electronic-structure methods that explicitly include local dynamic correlations (for example renormalized-band approaches or dynamical mean-field theory (DMFT) with an appropriate impurity solver and self-consistency) are required to obtain a quantitative description.
